moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH due to their downtrending status and exit scanner flags.
Exit all leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, to eliminate high-risk exposure as per conservative mode.
Add XLF, a top-quality financial sector setup, to diversify and improve overall portfolio quality.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall equity exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and low drawdown over aggressive growth.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces overall risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and downtrending positions. Expect lower volatility but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% concentration limit and rebalance broad index exposure.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH as they are downtrending and flagged by the exit scanner.
Exit SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with a risk-first growth approach.
Add AMD, a high-scoring tech setup, to introduce quality growth.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer while blending broad index and quality tech.
This approach aims for growth while keeping overall risk in check.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces concentration and leverage while adding quality growth setups. Expect moderate volatility and a balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% concentration mandate and free up capital for high-conviction trades.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH as they are downtrending and flagged, adhering to strict discipline.
Maintain a small allocation to SOXL, a leveraged ETF, to capitalize on potential upside in semiconductors.
Buy XOM, a top-scoring energy setup, to add diversification and strong risk/reward.
Buy AMD, a high-scoring tech setup, to press into working themes with strong scanner reads.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups for stretch growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It utilizes leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly increases both upside potential and drawdown risk. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily and can lead to substantial losses quickly without strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Reduce broad index for income focus
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to reduce broad index exposure and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Increase QQQI, an income-focused ETF, to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Sell QQQ and SMH due to their downtrending status and exit scanner flags.
Reduce CRWD, a high-beta single name, to lower overall portfolio volatility.
Exit all leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, to eliminate high-risk exposure.
This plan focuses on rotating into income and covered-call ETFs for yield.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, aiming for a low-drawdown profile. While distributions are sought, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and returns are not guaranteed.
